title: Watering Tips
shortDescription: Keep your plants hydrated without overwatering.
details: Water most houseplants once a week, or when the top 1–2 inches of soil feels dry.
Always use pots with drainage holes to prevent root rot.
Reduce watering during winter when plant growth slows down.
Use room-temperature water for best results.

title: Sunlight Tips
shortDescription: Find the right balance of light for healthy growth.
details: Most indoor plants prefer bright, indirect sunlight.
Avoid placing plants directly under harsh midday sun—it can scorch the leaves.
Rotate your pots every few days for even growth on all sides.
Low-light plants like snake plants or pothos can survive in dim corners.

title: Fertilizing Tips
shortDescription: Feed your plants the nutrients they need to thrive.
details: Fertilize every 2–4 weeks during spring and summer, when plants are actively growing.
Use a balanced, water-soluble fertilizer (10-10-10 or 20-20-20).
Avoid fertilizing in winter months when growth is minimal.
Flush soil occasionally with clean water to remove excess salts.

title: Pruning & Cleaning
shortDescription: Keep your plants looking neat and encourage new growth.
details: Trim dead or yellowing leaves regularly to redirect energy to new growth.
Use clean, sharp scissors to prevent plant infections.
Wipe leaves gently with a damp cloth to remove dust and improve photosynthesis.

title: Potting & Soil Care
shortDescription: Healthy roots start with healthy soil.
details: Repot plants every 12–18 months to refresh soil and give roots more room.
Use well-draining potting mix suited to the plant type (succulent mix, orchid mix, etc.).
Add perlite or sand to improve soil aeration for plants that dislike wet roots.
